sources.name: ACCESS Support Drupal
sources.description: ACCESS Support website CMS - canonical storage for support content

mcp.notes: Backend canonical source; content exposed via specialized APIs and MCPs

use_cases: What content types are stored in the ACCESS Support CMS?
When was a specific piece of content last modified?
How many published nodes exist by content type?

relationships.description: Stores user accounts (Drupal users, linked to COManage)

sources.name: Affinity Groups
sources.description: Community groups organized by interest or domain

tools.name: search_affinity_groups
tools.description: Search and filter affinity groups

tools.name: get_affinity_group_kb
tools.description: Get knowledge base resources for a group

tools.name: get_affinity_group_events
tools.description: Get events associated with a group

use_cases: Which affinity groups are available for a specific research domain?
Who coordinates a particular affinity group?
What events are associated with an affinity group?
How can I join or contact an affinity group?

constraints.description: Mailing lists, private group flags, and membership lists are restricted and must not be exposed through public APIs or AI tools.

fields.name: category
fields.description: Whether this is an RP-specific or community group

fields.name: coordinator_id
fields.description: Group coordinator (exposed as name string in MCP)

fields.name: private_users
fields.description: List of users with access to private group

sources.name: Announcements

tools.name: search_announcements
tools.description: Search announcements with filters

tools.name: get_my_announcements
tools.description: Get announcements authored by current user

tools.name: create_announcement
tools.description: Create a new announcement

tools.name: update_announcement
tools.description: Update an existing announcement

tools.name: delete_announcement
tools.description: Delete an announcement

use_cases: What announcements have been published this week?
Are there any announcements related to a specific affinity group?
What resource provider updates have been shared recently?

fields.name: affiliation
fields.description: Whether this is an official ACCESS or community announcement

relationships.description: Announcements can be associated with an affinity group
Announcements can be tagged for categorization

sources.name: COManage/ACCESS Identity

mcp.notes: Identity data is sensitive and not suitable for AI/MCP exposure

use_cases: How many unique users are registered in ACCESS?
Which institutions have the most ACCESS users?
Is a specific user affiliated with a particular institution?

constraints.description: Contains PII (names, emails, ACCESS IDs). Must not be exposed through public APIs, AI tools, or MCP servers.
Identity data may only be accessed by named individuals with legitimate need or systems with explicit authorization.
Subject to institutional data handling agreements and FERPA considerations for student researchers.

relationships.description: Users can be members of affinity groups

sources.name: Content API

fields.name: id
fields.description: Drupal node ID of the page.

fields.name: path
fields.description: Absolute URL of the support page.

fields.name: content_type
fields.description: Drupal content type of the page.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Full extracted plain text of the page (detail endpoint).

fields.name: content_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of the extracted text for incremental ingestion.

fields.name: content_url
fields.description: URL of the per-page content endpoint (index entries).

sources.description: Plain-text page content and a discovery index for ACCESS Support pages, for RAG ingestion and search syndication.
sources.notes: Responses include content_hash (SHA-256 of extracted text) and last_modified for incremental ingestion. Documented on the portal at /api-docs/content.
sources.query_capacity: Per-page by node ID or path alias

sources.name: Event Registrations
sources.description: Registration and attendance data for events

mcp.notes: Contains PII; partial coverage via events MCP

use_cases: How many people registered for a specific event?
What is the attendance rate for training sessions?
Which institutions are most represented at ACCESS events?
How do registrants hear about ACCESS events?

constraints.description: Contains PII (names, emails, ACCESS IDs). Must not be exposed through public APIs or AI tools.
Registration data may only be used for event management, reporting, and outreach effectiveness analysis. Individual-level data must not be shared outside authorized teams.

fields.name: referral_source
fields.description: How the registrant heard about the event

relationships.description: Each registration is for a specific event
Each registration is linked to a user

sources.name: Events and Training
sources.description: Workshops, webinars, training sessions, and office hours

tools.name: search_events
tools.description: Search events with filters for type, date, skill level

tools.name: get_event_by_id
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific event

tools.name: get_events_by_tag
tools.description: Get events filtered by tag

use_cases: What training sessions are available for beginners this month?
Which events are associated with a specific affinity group?
How many office hours are scheduled for next week?
What events require registration?

fields.name: affiliation
fields.description: Whether this is an official ACCESS or community event

fields.name: starts_in_hours
fields.description: Hours until event starts (negative if past)

fields.name: registration_url
fields.description: Link to register for the event

relationships.description: Events can be associated with an affinity group

sources.name: Resource Documentation API

fields.name: nid
fields.description: Drupal node ID of the resource.

fields.name: content_hash
fields.description: Deterministic SHA-256 fingerprint of the resource payload (detail endpoint) for change detection.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Canonical URL of the resource documentation page.

sources.description: Team-authored documentation for ACCESS resource providers (login, file transfer, storage, queue specs, top software, datasets), with resource-group inheritance.
sources.notes: Detail responses include a content_hash for change detection. Documented on the portal at /api-docs/resources.

sources.name: Resource Information (CIDeR)
sources.description: Organizations, resource descriptions, and integration information for ACCESS resource providers.
sources.notes: Source of record for ACCESS resource/organization data. The Resource Documentation API and SDS draw on CiDeR resource identifiers.

sources.name: SDS (Software Discovery Service)

fields.name: rps
fields.description: Request param: array of Resource Provider names/IDs to filter by, case-insensitive. Conditional (rps and/or software).

fields.name: software
fields.description: Request param: array of software names to filter by, case-insensitive. Conditional (rps and/or software).

fields.name: columns
fields.description: Request param: array of fields to return. rp_software and software_name are always included.

fields.name: exclude
fields.description: Request param: when true, treat columns as an exclude list rather than an include list. Default false.

fields.name: fuzz_software
fields.description: Request param: enable fuzzy matching on software names. Default false.

fields.name: fuzz_rp
fields.description: Request param: enable fuzzy matching on RP names/IDs. Default false.

fields.name: collapse_resource_groups
fields.description: Request param: when false, emit separate entries per resource group. Default true.

fields.name: software_name
fields.description: Response field: name of the software package. Results are sorted alphabetically by this field.

fields.name: rp_software
fields.description: Response field: the software identifier as known to the resource provider. Always present in responses.

fields.name: resource_provider
fields.description: Response field: resource provider / resource group the software is available on.

sources.description: Software discovery: which software packages are available on which ACCESS resource providers.
sources.notes: software-discovery MCP exists. Discovery is public; the API (POST /api/v1) is key-gated, request a key via https://support.access-ci.org/help-ticket. Documented at /api-docs/sds.

sources.name: XDMoD Metrics
sources.description: HPC metrics, usage analytics, and resource specifications from the ACCESS XDMoD instance

tools.name: describe_realms
tools.description: List all available data realms with dimensions and statistics counts

tools.name: describe_fields
tools.description: Get dimensions and statistics for a specific realm

tools.name: get_dimension_values
tools.description: Get filter values for a dimension (e.g., all resources, institutions)

tools.name: get_chart_data
tools.description: Get chart data and metadata for a statistic with optional filters

tools.name: get_chart_image
tools.description: Get chart as SVG, PNG, or PDF image

tools.name: get_chart_link
tools.description: Generate a URL to the interactive XDMoD portal chart

mcp_authenticated.notes: Authenticated access via XDMoD API token (xdmod-data Python package). Required for per-user data queries.

tools.name: get_user_data
tools.description: Get user-specific usage data by exact user identifier

tools.name: get_raw_data
tools.description: Extract XDMoD metrics with complex filtering, timeseries/aggregate modes

tools.name: describe_fields
tools.description: Discover dimensions and metrics in a specific realm

tools.name: describe_realms
tools.description: List all available XDMoD data realms with capabilities

tools.name: get_smart_filters
tools.description: Discover filter values for use with get_raw_data (resources, queues, institutions)

tools.name: get_analysis_template
tools.description: Get pre-configured analysis patterns (14 templates for common scenarios)

tools.name: integrate_nsf_xdmod
tools.description: Correlate NSF funding data with XDMoD computational usage patterns

use_cases: How much CPU time was used across ACCESS last quarter?
Which resources have the highest GPU utilization?
How many jobs ran on Delta in January 2024?
What science gateways are most active?
How are allocations distributed across fields of science?
What is the total GPU capacity across ACCESS resources?
Show me wait times by resource for the past month
How many active users and PIs are there?

realms.name: Accounts
realms.description: ACCESS user account tracking — accounts associated with allocations and job activity

statistics.name: unique_account_with_jobs_count
statistics.description: Number of Accounts Created with Jobs

realms.name: Allocations
realms.description: Allocation and project tracking — active allocations, PIs, and resource usage in SUs/ACEs

realms.name: Cloud
realms.description: Cloud and virtualized compute environment metrics

statistics.name: cloud_avg_cores_reserved
statistics.description: Average Cores Reserved Weighted By Wall Hours

statistics.name: cloud_avg_memory_reserved
statistics.description: Average Memory Reserved Weighted By Wall Hours (Bytes)

statistics.name: gateway_session_count
statistics.description: Number of Sessions Ended via Gateway

realms.name: Gateways
realms.description: Science gateway job metrics — jobs submitted through ACCESS gateways

statistics.name: normalized_avg_processors
statistics.description: Job Size Normalized (% of Total Cores)

statistics.name: avg_ace

realms.name: Jobs
realms.description: Job accounting and resource usage metrics from job schedulers

statistics.name: normalized_avg_processors
statistics.description: Job Size Normalized (% of Total Cores)

statistics.name: avg_ace
statistics.description: ACCESS Credit Equivalents Charged Per Job (SU)

realms.name: ResourceSpecifications
realms.description: Resource hardware specifications — CPU/GPU counts, node hours, and capacity metrics

statistics.name: total_avg_number_of_cpu_cores
statistics.description: Average Number of CPU Cores Total

statistics.name: allocated_avg_number_of_cpu_cores
statistics.description: Average Number of CPU Cores Allocated

statistics.name: total_avg_number_of_cpu_nodes
statistics.description: Average Number of CPU Nodes Total

statistics.name: allocated_avg_number_of_cpu_nodes
statistics.description: Average Number of CPU Nodes Allocated

statistics.name: total_avg_number_of_gpu_nodes
statistics.description: Average Number of GPU Nodes Total

statistics.name: allocated_avg_number_of_gpu_nodes
statistics.description: Average Number of GPU Nodes Allocated

realms.name: Storage
realms.description: File system and storage usage metrics

realms.name: SUPREMM
realms.description: Detailed job performance analytics — CPU, GPU, memory, network, and I/O metrics from monitoring

statistics.name: avg_percent_cpu_user
statistics.description: Avg CPU % User weighted by core-hour

statistics.name: avg_percent_cpu_system
statistics.description: Avg CPU % System weighted by core-hour

statistics.name: avg_percent_cpu_idle
statistics.description: Avg CPU % Idle weighted by core-hour

statistics.name: avg_percent_gpu_usage
statistics.description: Avg GPU usage weighted by GPU hour (%)

statistics.name: avg_flops_per_core
statistics.description: Avg FLOPS Per Core weighted by core-hour (ops/s)

statistics.name: avg_memory_per_core
statistics.description: Avg Memory Per Core weighted by core-hour (bytes)

statistics.name: avg_total_memory_per_core
statistics.description: Avg Total Memory Per Core weighted by core-hour (bytes)

statistics.name: avg_max_memory_per_core
statistics.description: Avg Max Memory weighted by core-hour (%)

statistics.name: avg_mem_bw_per_core
statistics.description: Avg Memory Bandwidth Per Core weighted by core-hour (bytes/s)

statistics.name: avg_ib_rx_bytes
statistics.description: Avg InfiniBand rate Per Node weighted by node-hour (bytes/s)

statistics.name: avg_ace
statistics.description: ACCESS Credit Equivalents Charged Per Job (SU)

notes: All statistics are available via public_user=true (no authentication required for aggregate data)
Personal/per-user data requires XDMoD API token authentication (xdmod-data Python package)
Statistics discovered from live API on 2026-02-22 using metaData.fields from get_data endpoint
SUPREMM has ~20 additional network I/O statistics (eth0, ib0, lustre, block device rates) not listed individually
Storage realm does not appear in menu queries but statistics work via direct get_data calls
